candidates.career_narrative: Anthony Serhan, CFA, is Distribution Director at Pendal Group, a position he has held since October 2018 following the firm's appointment of him as head of distribution. With approximately eight years in the role, he sits on the Australian Executive team and draws upon extensive experience across both retail and institutional funds management. He is responsible for strategic development of key relationships across the distribution platform, though precise reporting structures and team size remain to be verified.
candidates.firm_aum_context: Pendal Group is an established asset manager and distribution platform with presence across institutional and retail channels; exact AUM requires direct verification.

mobility_signal.rationale: Eight-year tenure in current role suggests deep embeddedness at Pendal Group. No recent public signals of transition or role exploration were identified in the research package. Mobility intent cannot be confirmed from available evidence and should be explored directly.

role_fit.justification: Serhan holds a Distribution Director title at an established funds-management firm with verified experience spanning both institutional and retail channels, which aligns well with the requirement for 10+ years in Australian distribution. His executive-level position and responsibility for strategic relationship development indicate senior leadership capability. However, public evidence does not confirm direct management of a national sales team, quantified revenue ownership, or specific networks across superannuation funds and dealer groups—key role requirements. His CFA designation supports strong investment product knowledge. The lack of verified detail on team size, platform relationships, and institutional client portfolio limits a higher score.

candidates.outreach_hook: Your track record building strategic relationships across institutional and retail channels at Pendal Group caught our attention for a distribution leadership opportunity with a growing mid-tier active manager.

current_role.title: Global Head of Distribution and Marketing

candidates.career_narrative: Lauren Prendiville was appointed Global Head of Distribution and Marketing at First Sentier Group in January 2025, based in Singapore. In this role she oversees global sales, client relationships, and marketing activities across the firm's distribution platform. Her tenure in the current role is estimated at approximately one year based on available public records, though precise chronology and prior career arc require further verification. She is responsible for global oversight of sales and client relationships at an established asset manager, though exact team size and revenue scope remain to be confirmed.
candidates.firm_aum_context: First Sentier Group appears in public records as an established asset manager and distribution platform, but exact AUM and institutional scale still require direct verification.

mobility_signal.rationale: Lauren was appointed to her current global role in January 2025, suggesting she is in the early phase of a significant new mandate. Tenure of approximately one year and the seniority of the appointment indicate low near-term mobility appetite, though public signals of active transition intent have not been observed and would require direct exploration.

role_fit.justification: Lauren holds a global distribution and marketing leadership role at an established funds-management platform, demonstrating seniority and responsibility for sales and client relationships. However, several dimensions require validation: her role is globally scoped rather than Australia-focused, and direct evidence of Australian institutional networks, platform relationships, or experience managing national sales teams in the local market is not yet visible in public records. The role specification calls for deep Australian networks across super funds, family offices, and dealer groups, as well as a proven track record in the domestic wholesale and institutional market—elements that cannot be confirmed from the current research package. While her global mandate suggests relevant functional capability, geographic fit and local market profile remain open questions.

candidates.outreach_hook: Lauren, your recent appointment to lead global distribution at First Sentier Group caught my attention, and I'd welcome a conversation about how your international platform experience might translate to a high-impact Australian market opportunity.

current_role.title: Head of Distribution Wealth Management Australia and New Zealand

candidates.career_narrative: Camelia Seric is Head of Distribution Wealth Management Australia and New Zealand at Merricks Capital, a funds-management firm with established distribution infrastructure. She has held this role for approximately four years, based on dated public-web evidence that should still be verified manually. Her current mandate spans wealth-channel distribution across ANZ, with visible team-building activity including the appointment of a Director, Distribution from Pinnacle Investment Management.
candidates.firm_aum_context: Merricks Capital appears in public-web results as an established investment or wealth platform, but exact AUM still requires direct verification.

mobility_signal.rationale: Four-year tenure with recent team expansion suggests ongoing investment in her current mandate; no public signals of active transition intent, though tenure length positions her within a plausible career-move window if the right opportunity arose.

role_fit.justification: Camelia's current title and scope align well with the Head of Distribution brief: she already leads wealth distribution nationally across ANZ and has demonstrable team-building experience. Her platform includes institutional and wholesale exposure, though the precise split between wealth, platform, and institutional channels is not fully documented in available evidence. Exact AUM context and depth of super-fund or family-office networks remain to be confirmed in conversation, but her role level and regional remit are a strong directional match.

candidates.outreach_hook: Camelia, your work building the wealth distribution function at Merricks Capital over the past four years caught our attention—I'd welcome a confidential conversation about a Head of Distribution opportunity with a mid-tier active manager scaling their institutional and wholesale presence.

candidates.career_narrative: Luke Cheetham currently serves as Director Distribution at PM Capital, an established asset manager, where he leads retail distribution efforts including engagement with licensee networks and intermediary channels. Public evidence confirms his focus on retail distribution and platform relationships, though precise tenure in the current role requires verification. His background demonstrates hands-on experience in distribution and sales within the Australian funds-management sector.
candidates.firm_aum_context: PM Capital appears in public-web results as an established investment or wealth platform, but exact AUM still requires direct verification.

mobility_signal.rationale: Tenure in current role could not be verified from available evidence, and no explicit public signals of transition intent were observed. Mobility assessment remains directional pending confirmation of time in seat and career trajectory.

role_fit.justification: Cheetham holds a director-level distribution title at an established funds-management firm and demonstrates operational exposure to retail and intermediary channels, including licensee networks and platform engagement. His current remit as Head of Retail Distribution suggests channel leadership capability. However, the role specification prioritizes institutional and wholesale coverage—superannuation funds, family offices, and institutional investors—while public evidence supports primarily retail and intermediary distribution experience. Direct evidence of institutional relationship ownership, national team leadership scale, and wholesale network depth could not be verified from available sources. He presents a credible adjacent candidate if retail-to-wholesale transition or dual-channel scope can be confirmed through interview.

candidates.outreach_hook: I noticed your work leading retail distribution at PM Capital, and I'd welcome a conversation about a national distribution leadership opportunity with broader institutional and wholesale scope.

candidates.career_narrative: Anthony Cochran is Head of Distribution at SG Hiscock & Company, a boutique Australian asset manager, where he has spent approximately seven years building the firm's distribution capability. He joined from Macquarie, bringing institutional and wholesale experience to the role. Public evidence confirms he has led distribution strategy across the firm's funds management platform, though precise details of team size and reporting lines require verification.
candidates.firm_aum_context: SG Hiscock & Company is an established Australian boutique asset manager and funds-management firm, though exact AUM still requires direct verification.

mobility_signal.rationale: Seven-year tenure at SG Hiscock suggests deep organisational commitment and limited public signals of active transition intent, though recent LinkedIn activity may indicate engagement with the broader market.

role_fit.justification: Anthony's current title and employer align closely with the mandate: he already leads distribution for a boutique Australian asset manager and brings a Macquarie pedigree that suggests strong institutional and wholesale networks. His circa seven-year tenure demonstrates staying power and likely deep market relationships across platforms, super funds, and advisers. The primary unknowns are verifiable AUM scale at SG Hiscock (which may be below the $5–20B target range) and confirmed evidence of managing a national sales team, though his Head of Distribution remit strongly implies both. Fit is strong on role scope and channel experience; firm scale and team leadership detail require follow-up.

candidates.outreach_hook: Your progression from Macquarie into the Head of Distribution seat at SG Hiscock caught our attention—keen to explore how your boutique-builder experience maps to a scaled national distribution mandate.
